title: Crispy Fluffy Vietnamese Cocoa Flatbread
description: A quick, fermented flatbread that marries Vietnamese flair with a chocolate twist, perfect for a paleo-friendly snack.

ingredients.name: Probiotic coconut yogurt
ingredients.notes: Provides a subtle fermentation for fluffiness

instructions: Whisk the almond flour, cocoa powder, coconut sugar, baking soda, and sea salt in a large bowl until fully combined.
In a separate bowl, blend coconut milk, coconut oil, vanilla extract (if using), and probiotic coconut yogurt until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a soft dough forms; it should be slightly sticky but manageable.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the dough to relax.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease the air fryer basket with coconut oil to prevent sticking.
Divide the dough into 6 equal portions and roll each into a 4-inch (10 cm) circle on a parchment‑lined surface; you should have 6 flatbreads.
Place the flatbreads in a single layer in the air fryer basket, ensuring they do not touch; cook for 4 minutes on one side.
Flip each flatbread and cook for an additional 3–4 minutes until crisp edges form and the center is cooked through.
Remove the flatbreads from the air fryer, let them cool on a rack for a couple of minutes, then serve warm or at room temperature. Enjoy as a sweet snack or alongside a savory dish.
